purpose: Provide a reusable internal DO-178C/DO-331 Audit Hub that indexes authoritative lifecycle artifacts into immutable evidence snapshots, exposes phase-oriented evidence exploration and traceability, supports auditable findings and review workflows without changing source artifacts, and opens a rich synthetic sample workspace whenever no real project is configured.
outcomes: Let engineers and auditors reconstruct a complete requirement-to-result evidence chain in under two minutes.
Expose missing, stale, inconsistent, failed, or unreviewed evidence before formal lifecycle reviews and audits.
Give every lifecycle area a consistent phase-specific explorer with dossiers, provenance, traces, findings, reviews, and baseline comparison.
Make the full product immediately evaluable with a deterministic, resettable sample that requires no MATLAB installation, network, or configured project.
Produce reproducible audit snapshots and synthetic-watermarked evidence packages tied to exact source revisions.
Keep authoritative requirements, models, code, tests, configuration records, and certification artifacts read-only.

actors.text: Internal or external auditor with approved access

goals.text: Assess lifecycle and objective-profile readiness from one evidence baseline without claiming certification.
Follow bidirectional traces across plans, requirements, models, data dictionaries, code, reviews, tests, results, coverage, problem reports, and certification records.
Find broken links, stale evidence, coverage gaps, failed tests, missing independence, obsolete references, and unclosed corrective actions.
Open a complete evidence dossier for any indexed artifact with provenance, revision, relationships, reviews, findings, and source location.
Compare retained baselines and understand change impact across all lifecycle areas.
Assign, disposition, correct, reverify, and close hub-local audit findings while preserving a complete activity history.
Assemble a reproducible audit package from selected evidence, findings, reviews, and objective mappings.
Explore every main workflow immediately in a realistic built-in sample workspace.

useCases.text: Create, select, and inspect a project workspace, objective profile, contributing repositories, artifact locations, extraction health, exact revision set, and current baseline.

When no real project is configured, automatically open the built-in AeroNav Flight Guidance Computer — Lateral Guidance sample at baseline 2.4.0 with a persistent Sample Data badge and a prominent Connect a real project action.
Open an overview showing lifecycle readiness, evidence inventory, changes since baseline 2.3.0, open findings, ingestion health, and cross-phase evidence flow.
Use the Evidence Explorer Planning area to inspect plans, standards, objective-profile applicability, approvals, deviations, reviews, and findings.
Use the Requirements area to inspect system and software requirement sets, hierarchy, derived requirements, quality checks, bidirectional traces, reviews, and findings.
Use the Design area to inspect architecture, Simulink and Stateflow model structure, interfaces, data dictionaries, design analyses, model reviews, traces, and findings.

Use the Implementation area to inspect C and H source inventory, functions, generated-versus-handwritten code, interfaces, build metadata, standards evidence, reviews, traces, and findings without providing a full IDE.
Use the Verification area to inspect verification matrices, test definitions, executions, result sets, model and code coverage, analyses, review evidence, failures, and findings.
Use the Configuration Management area to inspect baselines, change records, problem reports, build and release records, configuration status accounting, integrity checks, and findings.
Use the Quality Assurance area to inspect process audits, conformance records, independence evidence, deviations, corrective actions, reviews, and findings.

Use the Certification area to inspect objective cross-references, audit packages, authority questions, open items, accomplishment-summary evidence, and findings without reproducing licensed standard text.

Open an evidence dossier from any table, graph, metric, change, or finding and inspect metadata, authoritative-source provenance and hash, incoming and outgoing traces, reviews, findings, history, and package membership.

Trace a complete representative chain from SYS-LAT-014 through SWR-HLR-LAT-021, SWR-LLR-LAT-044, LateralGuidance/BankAngleLimiter, lateral_guidance.c::limit_bank_command, TC-LAT-BOUNDARY-008, RequirementsResults_2_4_0, and VR-RESULT-2026-041.

Detect and navigate an intentionally incomplete evidence chain, broken links, orphaned items, stale hashes, obsolete baseline references, missing reviews, failed tests, unverified problem reports, and coverage gaps.
Search and filter across IDs, titles, artifact types, lifecycle areas, repositories, revisions, owners, statuses, severities, review state, and source files, then pivot into traces or dossiers.
Compare baselines 2.3.0 and 2.4.0 to show added, removed, changed, and stale artifacts, relationships, results, reviews, coverage, findings, and impacted downstream evidence.

Create a hub-local finding from evidence, assign an owner and due date, record disposition and corrective action, attach review evidence, mark ready for closure, reverify, and close it with a tamper-evident history.
Inspect review evidence and independence, record hub-local review decisions and comments, and relate them to artifacts and findings without changing authoritative source files.

Run or inspect a refresh that scans and classifies supported artifacts, records extraction diagnostics, normalizes evidence, evaluates deterministic rules, and publishes a new immutable snapshot only when processing is reliable.

Create and export a deterministic audit package containing a manifest, selected dossiers, trace matrices, review evidence, findings, baseline metadata, hashes, and a synthetic-sample watermark when applicable.
Edit findings, reviews, package selections, dispositions, and display preferences in a sandbox overlay for the sample workspace, then reset all sample changes to the bundled deterministic state.

scenarios.text: A first-time user launches with no projects and immediately sees the complete sample overview rather than a blank state or blocking picker.
A V&V engineer follows the known complete lateral-guidance evidence chain and opens each artifact dossier.
A QA engineer opens a broken-link finding, assigns it, records corrective evidence, marks it ready for closure, reverifies it, and closes it.
A certification lead moves through every lifecycle area and reviews phase-specific readiness, unresolved findings, reviews, and objective mappings.
An engineer compares 2.3.0 with 2.4.0 and identifies the effect of bank-angle-limit and navigation-mode-transition changes.
A refresh encounters a corrupt or unsupported artifact; explicit diagnostics are shown and the last valid snapshot remains current.
A project administrator connects a real project; later launches open the last real project by default while the sample remains available under Examples.
An auditor exports a selected package and verifies its manifest, revision set, content hashes, finding state, and synthetic watermark.

information.text: Project identity, DO-178C software level, DO-331 applicability, lifecycle configuration, objective-profile reference, repositories, and artifact locations
Immutable snapshot and baseline identity, exact repository commits, branch or tag context, timestamps, tool versions, extraction version, and content hashes
Plans, standards, checklists, approvals, deviations, objective applicability, process records, and accomplishment-summary references
System requirements, high-level requirements, low-level requirements, derived requirements, classifications, attributes, hierarchy, and requirement reviews from .slreqx extractions
Typed incoming and outgoing trace relationships from .slmx and other configured link sources

Architecture, Simulink and Stateflow models, model elements, interfaces, parameters, variants, data dictionaries, analyses, and review evidence from .slx, .sldd, .slreqx, .m, and normalized extractions
C and H files, generated and handwritten classification, functions, interfaces, build units, coding-standard results, code reviews, and source hashes

Verification cases, procedures, harnesses, simulations, executions, result sets, iterations, expected and actual values, verdicts, anomalies, and reviews from .mldatx, .xlsx, and normalized result extracts
Imported model and code coverage summaries including statement, decision, condition, and MC/DC where applicable
Reviewer, independence role, scope, artifact revision, checklist, comments, decisions, approvals, dates, and evidence attachments
Finding type, severity, lifecycle area, affected evidence, owner, due date, status, disposition, corrective action, re-verification, closure, and immutable activity history
Baselines, change requests, problem reports, build records, releases, configuration indexes, integrity checks, and status-accounting records
Quality audits, conformance results, deviations, corrective actions, independence evidence, and approvals
Configurable objective identifiers and mappings, audit packages, authority questions, open items, and accomplishment-summary evidence without bundled copyrighted objective text

Synthetic AeroNav Flight Guidance Computer — Lateral Guidance dataset with components Mode Manager, Lateral Guidance, and Command Monitor; baselines 2.3.0 and 2.4.0; deterministic people, dates, identifiers, hashes, and relationships

Sample inventory: 10 plans and standards, 18 system requirements, 36 HLRs, 54 LLRs, 6 derived requirements, 7 models or harnesses, 2 data dictionaries, about 140 model elements, 24 C/H files, about 105 functions, 64 tests, about 225 iterations, 6 result sets, about 45 reviews, about 18 change or problem records, 12 findings, and 2 baselines

Twelve sample findings covering derived-requirement feedback, a broken LLR-to-model link, stale data-dictionary review, missing code trace, robustness-test failure, stale result hash, decision-coverage gap, missing independence evidence, obsolete configuration-index reference, unreverified problem report, approved standards deviation, and a finding ready for closure

rules.text: Connected engineering artifacts remain authoritative; the hub must not write changes back to requirements, trace, model, dictionary, source, test, result, spreadsheet, review, configuration, quality, or certification files.
Only hub-local findings, reviews, dispositions, package selections, annotations, and sample sandbox state are editable, and every change is auditable.
Each published snapshot is immutable and identifies exact source revisions, extraction context, and hashes.
Required extraction and indexing failures prevent candidate publication; evidence gaps remain visible findings and do not by themselves suppress a reliable snapshot.
A failed or partial refresh never replaces the last valid published snapshot.
Deterministic findings are recalculated per snapshot; human findings retain attribution, history, disposition, and closure evidence.
Evidence is stale when its recorded source revision or dependency hash differs from the selected snapshot, or when a dependency changed without required downstream regeneration or review.
The Evidence Explorer navigation is Overview, Planning, Requirements, Design, Implementation, Verification, Configuration Management, Quality Assurance, and Certification.
Every lifecycle area uses a consistent phase header, tailored subnavigation, working evidence table or visualization, evidence inspector, findings drawer, and baseline-compare mode.

Every applicable evidence item supports opening its dossier, following traces, opening a source location, comparing baselines, inspecting provenance and hash, reviewing related findings and reviews, creating a hub-local finding, and adding it to a package.
If zero real projects exist, the sample opens automatically with no picker blocker or blank dashboard.
After a real project exists, the last real project opens by default and the sample remains selectable under Examples.
The sample always displays a Sample Data badge and a Connect a real project action.
Bundled sample evidence is immutable; sample findings, reviews, dispositions, packages, and preferences are stored in a resettable sandbox overlay.
Every sample export is marked Synthetic sample — not certification evidence in both visible output and manifest metadata.
The sample uses a normalized extraction snapshot representing synthetic proprietary-format artifacts; it must not disguise text or JSON as .slreqx, .slmx, .slx, .sldd, or .mldatx binaries.
Sample and real projects use the same canonical APIs, domain model, routes, and components; there is no demo-only parallel interface.
Do not bundle or reproduce copyrighted DO-178C or DO-331 objective text; use configurable identifiers and organization-provided licensed profiles.
The application may report evidence status against the configured profile but must not claim certification, regulatory approval, or compliance.
Requirement classifications and allocation rules prevent ambiguous duplication between higher-level and low-level requirement views.
Coverage is imported from authoritative results; the hub does not calculate coverage.
Baseline comparison reports added, removed, changed, and stale evidence without performing semantic merge or conflict resolution.
Credentials and secrets are never stored in project configuration, snapshots, logs, packages, or displayed evidence.
Normal browsing, filtering, search, trace navigation, dossier opening, and phase switching should usually respond within two seconds at accepted scale.
The bundled sample has deterministic identifiers, dates, people, revisions, hashes, relationships, counts, and reset behavior.

externalSystems.text: Configured local or company-controlled Git workspaces and file stores containing plans, .slreqx, .slmx, .slx, .sldd, .m, .c, .h, .mldatx, .xlsx, coverage, review, CM, QA, and certification evidence
Git repository operations for exact revision resolution, baseline metadata, and optional source hyperlinks
Optional licensed MATLAB, Simulink, Simulink Requirements, and Simulink Test tooling or organization-provided extractors for real proprietary artifacts
Local filesystem destination for deterministic audit package exports

constraints.text: Medium-sized reusable desktop or local web application with a complete front end and realistic in-memory or local sample behavior.
The built-in sample requires no MATLAB installation, network, external service, credential, or repository.
Real-project ingestion supports configured normalized extracts and may use licensed tooling when available.
A real project may aggregate up to 5 repositories and approximately 1,000 requirements, 250,000 model or code elements, 5,000 tests, 1,000,000 trace links, and 2,000 retained snapshots.
The surrounding company environment controls access; the first release does not implement application-level identity or authorization.
The UI is dark-first, dense but legible, keyboard accessible, responsive at common desktop widths, and uses no empty placeholder pages.
The code view presents searchable metadata and focused excerpts only, not an IDE.
Refresh and extraction may be asynchronous and may take longer than interactive operations.
Earlier published snapshots are retained unless removed outside the application by authorized infrastructure.
The hub does not trigger CI or Azure DevOps pipelines.

inScope: Evidence dossiers and cross-phase trace graph
Immutable snapshots and 2.3.0 versus 2.4.0 baseline comparison
Artifact ingestion inventory and extraction diagnostics
Deterministic and human findings with assignment, correction, reverification, and closure
Configurable objective-profile mappings without standard text
Source provenance, hashes, and native-tool or repository links where available
Responsive and keyboard-accessible complete front end
outOfScope: Editing or writing back to authoritative lifecycle artifacts
Bundling licensed DO-178C or DO-331 objective text
Generating genuine proprietary MATLAB binary fixtures without licensed tooling
Full IDE or embedded editable MATLAB views
Public internet or external-service dependency for the sample

acceptanceCases.description: Launch with no configured real project.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: AeroNav Flight Guidance Computer — Lateral Guidance baseline 2.4.0 opens automatically with complete overview content, Sample Data badge, and Connect a real project action.

acceptanceCases.description: Visit every Evidence Explorer lifecycle area.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: Overview, Planning, Requirements, Design, Implementation, Verification, Configuration Management, Quality Assurance, and Certification each contain meaningful phase-specific data, controls, visualizations, dossiers, reviews, and findings.
The user navigates through HLR, LLR, model element, C function, test case, result set, and verification record with provenance and hashes preserved.

acceptanceCases.description: Open the intentionally incomplete chain and each seeded issue.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The hub explains the precise break, staleness, failure, review, coverage, configuration, or closure condition and links to affected evidence.
The hub shows bank-angle-limit and navigation-mode-transition changes plus impacted artifacts, traces, reviews, tests, results, coverage, and findings.

acceptanceCases.description: Exercise global and phase-specific search and filters.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: Results update immediately and open the same canonical dossiers used elsewhere.

acceptanceCases.description: Drive one sample finding through its workflow.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: Assignment, corrective action, ready-for-closure, reverification, and closure are recorded with actor, timestamp, evidence, and immutable history.

acceptanceCases.description: Reset the sample workspace after edits.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: All overlay changes are removed and the deterministic bundled state is restored without changing sample evidence.

acceptanceCases.description: Assemble and export a sample audit package.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The package includes a deterministic manifest, selected evidence, trace and review summaries, finding state, baseline metadata, hashes, and the Synthetic sample — not certification evidence watermark.

The sample visibly represents plans, .slreqx, .slmx, .slx, .sldd, .m, .c, .h, .mldatx, .xlsx, coverage, review, CM, QA, and certification records as normalized synthetic evidence, without counterfeit binary files.

acceptanceCases.description: Connect or select a real project after using the sample.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The same routes and components render real normalized evidence; future launches prefer the last real project while Sample remains under Examples.

acceptanceCases.description: Attempt to mutate an authoritative evidence artifact.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: No such control exists; only hub-local workflow records and sample overlay state can change.

acceptanceCases.description: Simulate a corrupt or unsupported extraction input.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: Explicit diagnostics appear, the candidate is not published, and the last valid snapshot remains available.

acceptanceCases.description: Use keyboard navigation and resize the application across common desktop widths.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: All main views, phase navigation, tables, graphs, drawers, dialogs, and actions remain operable with no inaccessible or empty critical state.

acceptanceCases.description: Use the sample end to end offline.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: Every principal view and workflow operates without MATLAB, network access, credentials, or external services.

sources.text: Current workbench capability plan and project context
User-approved product discussion on 2026-07-22 covering use cases, lifecycle phase subviews, and detailed automatic sample fallback
FAA recognition boundary and official MathWorks artifact-format behavior considered during product definition
